title: Release agent-utils v1.0.2 for kitty image preview extension
description: Bump the package version and publish a new git tag so Pi users pinned to git:github.com/harryaskham/agent-utils can install the kitty image preview and Tendril screenshot capture extension changes.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:5nefmjf5xnxv9naq

title: Anchor kitty image preview with Unicode placeholders in tmux
description: Fix the kitty image preview widget so images render at the widget location and scroll with the pane, especially under tmux. Use kitty graphics Unicode placeholder placement rather than only emitting APC display commands, preserve non-tmux behavior, update docs, and validate protocol serialization.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:5nefmjf5xnxv9naq

title: Publish missing v1.0.3 git tag
description: bd-a71b61 is closed and origin/main contains landed commit 0ff97087c35671d6111adc35b33e49153d8950a0. The internal/origin checkout has refs/tags/v1.0.3 at 0ff97087c35671d6111adc35b33e49153d8950a0 after git fetch --tags, but GitHub API refs/tags/v1.0.3 returns 404 and GitHub tag listing only shows v1.0.2, v1.0.1, and v1. Publish/push the v1.0.3 tag to GitHub and verify gh api repos/harryaskham/agent-utils/git/ref/tags/v1.0.3 returns the landed SHA.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Add Pi kitty graphics image display extension
description: Implement a repo-local Pi extension/widget for persistent terminal image display using the kitty graphics protocol. Expose tools for Pi agents to add images or folders/series, navigate next/previous/indexed images, support autodetected streaming/in-memory/tmux passthrough paths, animations/transparency/z-index/background modes where feasible, and factor shared TypeScript kitty graphics protocol utilities for native image-posting integrations.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:5nefmjf5xnxv9naq

title: Add Tendril screenshot capture tool to kitty image preview extension
description: Extend the repo Pi kitty image preview extension with a first-party tool that captures the most recent/target screenshot through the tendril CLI into a session-designated folder and auto-shows it in the persistent kitty graphics preview widget. Include session-local screenshot storage, configurable tendril target/output options, docs, and validation.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:5nefmjf5xnxv9naq

title: Publish missing v1.0.2 git tag
description: Release bead bd-07984b closed and package.json on origin/main is version 1.0.2 at b844046, but after git fetch --tags the controller checkout does not see tag v1.0.2 (only v1.0.1/v1). Publish or repair the missing v1.0.2 git tag and verify it is visible from the canonical checkout/upstream.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Seed GitHub Pages with tool inventory
description:
Create an initial GitHub Pages site for this repository that publishes a concise inventory of the tools and first-party surfaces available to agents/operators.

Scope:
- Add/enable a minimal GitHub Pages-compatible docs site for the repo.
- Include a tool inventory covering the Cacophony surfaces used by this project, such as bd, agent, msg, scratch, test, build, choices, cron, image generation, Pi loop/self-nudge/self-ops, tmux-cli, Tendril, and relevant repo-local tools.
- Keep the inventory generated or easy to update where practical; avoid duplicating stale long-form docs unnecessarily.
- Document how to preview/build the Pages content locally.
- Ensure the site can be published by GitHub Pages without requiring secrets or local-only state.

Acceptance criteria:
- A docs or Pages entry point exists and renders a tool inventory.
- The repo documents the Pages publishing path and local preview/build command.
- Validation appropriate for the chosen static site setup passes.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Add fixed right-side kitty image preview placement
description: Extend the Pi kitty image preview extension with a fixed right-side overlay/column placement so screenshots can stay visible outside the scrolling editor area. Use non-capturing Pi overlays where available, force anchored Unicode placeholder rendering for the side placement, keep above/below widget behavior, update docs/tests, and release a new package tag.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:5nefmjf5xnxv9naq

title: Add kitty graphics-backed Pi theme package
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: investigate/implement shipping a Pi theme as part of the agent-utils Pi plugin/package, backed by the existing kitty graphics library with a TypeScript implementation.

Context / feasibility:
- Pi packages can ship themes: package.json supports a `pi.themes` entry and conventional `themes/` directories; packages can also ship extensions alongside themes.
- Pi themes themselves are JSON color-token definitions, but a theme+extension combination can provide richer custom rendering using the extension APIs.
- caco-tui has good Rust kitty graphics examples, but the Rust crate is not currently FFI/linkable/callable from Pi TypeScript, so implement a TypeScript renderer for now rather than trying to link the Rust crate.

Requested direction:
- Build a Pi package/extension/theme combination that uses agent-utils' kitty graphics protocol/library to render freeform graphical UI affordances.
- Prototype a small TypeScript renderer that can render lines/borders/styles into actual byte/image form suitable for kitty graphics display and Unicode placeholder placement.
- Use kitty Unicode placeholder characters to display graphical output at theme/UI locations, e.g. replacing simple ASCII prompt enclosure lines like `-----` with rendered graphics.
- Explore graphical/translucent/gradient borders around tables, agent/code blocks, or other message blocks.
- Keep the implementation in TypeScript for Pi; consult caco-tui Rust examples only as a reference for protocol/rendering behavior.
- Package any theme assets through `themes/` or package.json `pi.themes`, and any runtime behavior through the Pi extension package entries.
- Add docs/examples explaining how to install/select the theme and how the extension cooperates with the theme.
- Add tests for renderer byte output/protocol generation and for package manifest discovery where practical.

Important constraints:
- Do not globally clear/delete kitty graphics images; coordinate with bd-f89780's scoped image ownership/cleanup work.
- Avoid redrawing/destroying the Caco/Pi UI; all image placement/deletion should be scoped to the extension-owned images/placeholders.
- Preserve existing terminal/tmux placeholder behavior.

Controller triage notes:
- Pi docs confirm packages can ship themes via package.json `pi.themes` or conventional `themes/` directories.
- Duplicate searches for `kitty theme`, `unicode placeholder theme`, and `graphical borders` found no matching bead across all statuses.
- Main-history keyword check found prior kitty graphics/image preview work, but not this theme-backed graphical rendering feature.
- Depends on bd-f89780 because scoped image ownership/cleanup is prerequisite safety for using kitty graphics to decorate core UI regions.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Profile audit: controller reified instructions include worker lifecycle conflicts
description: Profile audit finding from pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl reified instructions. The actual reified instruction files above checkout combine generic worker lifecycle constraints with the project-controller persistent role. Generic Managed Agent Constraints say every managed agent is working an assigned bead, should start by running caco bd show, and when finished must commit and run caco agent complete. The project-controller profile for this same agent says it is a persistent controller, must not pick up worker tasks or implement application code, must not call caco agent complete, and should use reintegrate only for controller-owned operational changes. There is also recurring-task ambiguity: generic guidance says to use daemon-managed caco cron for recurring tasks, while project-controller/Pi guidance says per-controller self-check loops should stay inside the Pi runtime and not rely on daemon-global cron. Impact: persistent controllers receive contradictory lifecycle/startup/scheduling instructions and may mistakenly behave as bead workers. Please triage/profile-materialization so persistent controllers get role-specific lifecycle guidance without worker-only completion/hydration requirements and clarify scheduling precedence.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Improve kitty graphics multiviewer navigation and scoped cleanup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: improve the Pi kitty graphics image display extension/multiviewer.

Requested behavior:
- Add controls/commands for the multiviewer to move through images explicitly: e.g. /kitty-show-next and /kitty-show-prev.
- Add a timed cycling mode: e.g. /kitty-start-cycle 5 to cycle images every 5 seconds and /kitty-stop-cycle to stop. If there is an existing command naming convention, follow it, but provide equivalent next/previous/start/stop-cycle behavior.
- Make the multiviewer UI expose these controls clearly enough for users.
- Fix image cleanup/deletion: the plugin appears to be killing all kitty graphics images in the entire terminal. When run inside caco this redraws/destroys the entire UI. The extension must only manage/delete the kitty images it created.

Implementation guidance:
- Track image IDs / placements / namespaces owned by this extension, and scope clear/delete operations to those owned resources only.
- Avoid global kitty graphics clear/delete sequences that affect unrelated images or the caco UI.
- Preserve existing tmux/placeholder placement behavior and current preview commands.
- Add/update tests and docs for navigation, cycle controls, and scoped cleanup behavior.
- Verify with the project’s normal test suite or targeted tests if the full suite is not practical.

Controller triage notes:
- Existing closed bead bd-74597a covers the original Pi kitty graphics extension, not these multiviewer/cycle/scoped-cleanup changes.
- Search/main-history check did not find an already-landed multiviewer cycle/scoped cleanup change on main before filing this bead.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl
